seoTitle: How to Stretch an Image in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to stretch and manipulate images in Canva to fit your design layout while keeping quality in check.
intro: Sometimes an image just doesn't fit the space you need it to, and stretching is the quickest fix. This tutorial covers the technique for stretching an image in Canva, along with tips for keeping quality intact as you resize.

steps.heading: Select the image to stretch
steps.body: Choose the image within your Canva design that you want to adjust.

steps.heading: Apply the stretching technique
steps.body: Drag the image's edges or corners to stretch it to fit your layout, rather than scaling it proportionally.

steps.heading: Maintain image quality
steps.body: Check the stretched image for distortion or blurriness, adjusting as needed to keep it looking clean.

steps.heading: Apply this to branding and marketing projects
steps.body: Use stretched images carefully in flyers or social posts where a specific layout requires filling an unusual space.

faq.question: Does stretching an image distort its proportions?
faq.answer: Yes, stretching by dragging a single edge changes the image's proportions, unlike proportional resizing.

faq.question: How can I avoid a stretched image looking unnatural?
faq.answer: Stretch subtly and check the result carefully, since large distortions are usually noticeable and can look unprofessional.

faq.question: Is there a way to crop instead of stretch to fill a space?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva also offers cropping tools as an alternative to stretching when you want to fill a space without distortion.

faq.question: Can stretched images be used in professional branding materials?
faq.answer: It's best used sparingly and carefully, since noticeable distortion can undercut a professional, polished look.
